730	== Getting Started
731	
732	For builders and operators new to
733	{at_recipe_bottle},
734	this section provides conceptual grounding and the setup journey.
735	
736	include::RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc[leveloffset=+2]
737	
738	// ============================================================================
739	// TIER 2: HOW
740	// ============================================================================
741	
742	== Google API Engagement
743	
744	{at_recipe_bottle}
745	invokes
746	{gcp_service}
747	APIs through
748	{giam_service_account}
749	JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.
750	
751	* *Authentication*:
752	{giam_service_account_s}
753	use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
754	These JWTs are exchanged for
755	{oauth_token_s}
756	via Google's token endpoint.
757	All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.
758	
759	* *Invocation Pattern*:
760	Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
761	Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
762	Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.
763	
764	* *Long-Running Operations*:
765	{gcp_service}
766	{gcp_lro_s}
767	return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
768	The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
769	The system handles both success and error states.
770	Operations utilize
771	{rbbc_submit}
772	and
773	{rbbc_await}
774	to handle all asynchronous operations.
775	
776	* *IAM Policies*:
777	All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
778	{gcp_service}
779	resources.
780	
781	* *IAM Member Format*:
782	All IAM member bindings use `serviceAccount:«EMAIL»` format.
783	+
784	GCP IAM policy `members` fields only accept email-format identifiers (`serviceAccount:name@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).
785	The numeric `uniqueId` returned by `serviceAccounts.create` is valid for *API URL paths* (e.g., `projects/-/serviceAccounts/«UNIQUE_ID»` for GET/verification) but *not* for policy binding members.
786	`serviceAccount:«NUMERIC_ID»` returns HTTP 400 "Invalid service account" on all setIamPolicy endpoints — project, repository, and bucket alike.
787	+
788	Google's documentation recommends numeric IDs "to identify a service account just after it is created" (link:https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/manage-access-service-accounts[Manage access to service accounts]), but this applies to API resource addressing, not to IAM policy member strings.
789	The internal IAM system resolves email→numeric transparently; callers must use email.
790	+
791	Google-provisioned service agents (Cloud Build) are not affected — they are managed by Google, not newly created.
792	
793	== Operational Invariants
794	
795	=== Concurrent Session Safety
796	
797	{at_recipe_bottle}
798	commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
799	This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.
800	
801	Concurrency safety is achieved through:
802	
803	* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
804	Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BURD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.
805	
806	* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
807	Cloud resources (build IDs, inscribe timestamps) incorporate unique identifiers, preventing overwrite conflicts in
808	{rbtgi_repository}.
809	
810	* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
811	Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
812	The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.
813	
814	* *Semantic Preconditions*:
815	Operations like
816	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
817	require clean git state.
818	This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.
819	
820	NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
821	Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.
822	
823	NOTE: The
824	{gcb_service}
825	private pool enforces a *Concurrent Build CPUs (Private Pool)* quota per project per region.
826	Machine type is set at pool creation time via
827	{rbrd_gcb_machine_type}
828	(a Compute Engine type such as `e2-standard-2`); individual build requests do not specify machine type.
829	If the pool's vCPU allocation is nearly exhausted, a second
830	build invocation will remain in `QUEUED` status while the first is `WORKING`.
831	The
832	{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
833	regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
834	See
835	{rbtgo_quota_build}
836	for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.
837	
838	== Key Premises
839	
840	[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
841	//axl_voices axk_premise
842	{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
843	{at_recipe_bottle}
844	scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
845	All local
846	{gcp_service}
847	interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
848	+
849	**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.

6	{rbrv_regime}
7	instance represents a vessel image source: bound from an external registry, conjured from a Dockerfile, or grafted from a local build.

21	Optional; 0-512 characters.

35	Selects vessel provisioning strategy.

62	{rbrv_group_binding}
63

65	{rbrv_vessel_mode}

69	{rbrv_vessel_mode}

97	{rbrv_vessel_mode}

125	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
126	{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}

296	
297	**Operator federation — external-IdP identity for depot participants.**
298	Recipe Bottle grants
299	{rbtgi_depot}
300	capabilities to identities the depot's IAM recognizes.
301	The shipping model is the keyfile tier: each operator role holds a service-account key file (the
302	{rbtgi_rbra_file})
303	on disk, possession of the key is the identity, and no GCP organization is required.
304	Operator federation is the heavyweight successor tier for installations that cannot accept an operator secret on disk.
305	The depot owner configures the
306	{rbtgi_depot}
307	to trust an external OIDC/SAML identity provider (any compliant issuer — self-hosted Dex/Keycloak, or GCP Identity Platform as an in-house IdP), then grants capabilities to those federated `principal://` subjects: keyless, no Google accounts for participants, revocation central at the depot.
308	The
309	{rbtgi_depot}
310	remains the home of the participant roster (the role-to-identity mapping); the IdP owns only identity proofing.
311	Org-only — federation pools are organization-level (Workforce Identity Federation, the human-identity product), so a no-org
312	{rbtr_payor}
313	cannot use this tier; the organization requirement is the line between the free keyfile tier and the federation tier.
314	A qualifying organization is free via Cloud Identity once its owner verifies control of a DNS domain, so the true prerequisite is that the operator controls a domain — not that they pay Google.
315	The Google-side credential path is curl-native — an STS token exchange (RFC 8693), no gcloud and no added stack dependency; only the IdP-auth leg (device flow where the issuer supports it) varies by issuer.
316	Distinct from the envoy's egress federation (GCP-to-AWS delivery, the machine-identity Workload product): this is
317	{at_operator}
318	*access* identity arriving the other way.
319	_Trigger_: first concrete need from an organization that cannot accept on-disk operator keyfiles, or when the keyfile tier's secret-distribution and rotation burden materially blocks adoption rather than being theoretically frictionful.
320	_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260527-operator-credential-models.md` (the source document for this entry — the committed two-tier plan: keyfile (free, no-org) and Workforce federation (paid, org-required), the curl-native two-leg flow, device-flow / 12-hour session cap, the per-depot mode enum homed in RBRD, and the cult-verb shape);
321	`Memos/memo-20260522-org-affiliated-credential-reorientation.md` (R1/R2/R3 credential model and three-tier ladder, plus R4/R5 test-rig and gauntlet resolutions);
322	`Memos/memo-20260427-google-native-human-auth.md` (impersonation mechanism detail — its human-auth *framing* is superseded by R2);
323	Google docs: Workforce Identity Federation overview (`https://docs.cloud.google.com/iam/docs/workforce-identity-federation`) and short-lived token exchange (`https://docs.cloud.google.com/iam/docs/workforce-obtaining-short-lived-credentials`).

**Repair locations**:

The memo identifies bindings as one of three independent lifecycles; RBSCIG documents only GRANT. When a recently-deleted SA is still referenced in a policy, revoking the binding will fail with HTTP 400 "is not deleted" (class B backward propagation). The memo calls this H1's race: divest deletes the SA, but the binding revoke should run *before* delete, not after. 1. **RBSCIG revoke section**: Documents the API contract that makes teardown safe; without it, operators won't understand why revoke *before* delete matters (backward propagation race).

7. **RBSRD/RBSDD revoke steps**: Wire H1's never-called revoke half; without explicit revoke, divest-then-re-invest still leaks bindings.
